title: Tree Node
description: Classify each node as Root, Inner, or Leaf based on parent relationships.
constraints: p_id is NULL for the root node only — exactly one root exists
There are no cycles — the table represents a valid tree
key_concept: CASE WHEN with subqueries: Root (p_id IS NULL), Leaf (not in any p_id), Inner (rest).

hints.L1: Root: p_id is NULL. Leaf: id does not appear as any node's p_id. Inner: everything else.
hints.L2: CASE WHEN p_id IS NULL THEN 'Root' WHEN id NOT IN (SELECT p_id WHERE p_id IS NOT NULL) THEN 'Leaf' ELSE 'Inner' END
hints.L3: SELECT id, CASE WHEN p_id IS NULL THEN 'Root' WHEN id IN (SELECT DISTINCT p_id FROM Tree WHERE p_id IS NOT NULL) THEN 'Inner' ELSE 'Leaf' END AS type FROM Tree

optimal_solution:
SELECT id,
  CASE WHEN p_id IS NULL THEN 'Root'
       WHEN id IN (SELECT DISTINCT p_id FROM Tree WHERE p_id IS NOT NULL) THEN 'Inner'
       ELSE 'Leaf' END AS type
FROM Tree;
